candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line, but nothing indicates it is specifically related to COGS/materials and services. Using it for COGS would risk misallocating a company-wide operating D&A line.
Component of the total D&A line, but not labelled as COGS-related and inferior to the summary row for avoiding double counting.
Component of the total D&A line, but not labelled as COGS-related and inferior to the summary row for avoiding double counting.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is 'Materials and services'. No explicit D&A row is labelled as cost of sales / cost of revenues / materials and services, so there is insufficient evidence that any visible D&A should be added specifically to COGS. Conservative choice avoids double counting or misallocation.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A summary line shown above EBIT at the same level as operating expense buckets. It sits outside the selected XSGA components ('Employee benefits expenses' and 'Other operating expenses'), which were chosen with D&A intentionally excluded, so this is the row to add separately. Prefer the summary over component lines.
Part of the D&A breakdown beneath the summary line; should not be chosen when the summary row exists.
Granular component of amortization beneath the summary line; inferior to the summary row and would risk double counting.
Component of the total D&A line; inferior to the summary row and would risk double counting.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Very narrow component line, not suitable when an overall D&A summary row exists; also not mapped to XRD earlier.

variables.notes: OK. The selected XSGA bucket is the combination of 'Employee benefits expenses' and 'Other operating expenses'. 'Depreciation and amortization' appears as a separate operating line above EBIT rather than as a sub-component under those selected rows, so it should be added separately later. Component D&A rows are not selected because the summary row is present.
